The author argues they should be the default choice: appending STRICT to a table definition also blocks bogus column types like JSON, UUID, or DATETIME at creation time, while still allowing lossless conversions such as '123' into an INTEGER column.

title: How a designer created the "dirty, organic, insectoid logo" for Marvel Cosmic Invasion
blurb: Designer Stephane Perez (Mardoch) broke down how he built the Marvel Cosmic Invasion logo, splitting the wordmark into a retro-futuristic COSMIC drawn from '60s and '70s sci-fi title fonts and a raw, insectoid INVASION tied to the antagonist Annihilus. The write-up is a concrete look at treating a logo as narrative, with iteration passes and texture choices tied directly to the game's theme.

title: Ghost Font: A font that humans can read but AI cannot
blurb: Ghost Font is an experimental "anti-AI" typeface that renders text as motion, encoding each letter in the movement of dots so a human eye reads the message while a single paused frame or screenshot dissolves into static noise. Built as a browser prototype that runs locally and exports a shareable video clip, it revives designer Sang Mun's 2013 ZXX concept for an era where leading models decode OCR-resistant fonts with a single prompt.

title: Mesh-89 device lets Palestinian drivers stay ahead of checkpoint closures
blurb: Central Saint Martins graduate Joud Abuhomos designed Mesh-89, an off-grid device that alerts Palestinian bus drivers to the real-time status of the West Bank's 89 permanent checkpoints. The deliberately plain-looking unit uses mesh networking to route closure updates without cellular infrastructure, turning a graduate design project into a working tool for navigating daily movement restrictions.

title: Hovis and Kingsmill are now one business – and BrandOpus has given it an identity with no wheat in sight
blurb: Allied Bakeries completed its acquisition of Hovis Group, merging Hovis, Kingsmill, Allinson's and Sunblest into Hovis Bakeries, the UK's largest bread manufacturer at roughly 35% market share, with BrandOpus handling positioning and identity. The studio dropped the category's stock imagery of wheat fields, sliced loaves and sunrises for an "Elemental Power" system and a "Nourishing the Nation" line, betting on stature over nostalgia in a wrapped-bread market that has declined for over a decade.

title: How to build a digital painting from the ground up using Photoshop and Blender
blurb: Fantasy illustrator Pierre Ignaszewski walks through his process for a personal piece, blocking out architecture and mood in Blender before exporting to Photoshop to render the queen, creatures, and background front to back. The core discipline is legibility: establish global values, colors, and silhouettes first, reserve the widest value range and finest detail for the focal point, and simplify the background so it never competes.

title: Taste cannot be delegated
blurb: The author argues that taste cannot be encoded or delegated, because both AI models and consensus-driven organizations are good at recognizing what is preferred but incapable of deciding what deserves to exist. The essay ties two earlier pieces together, making the case that a designer's value now lives in the willingness to own a decision, not in producing or aggregating more options.

title: 'Ghostcommit' hides prompt injection in images to fool AI agents, steal secrets
blurb: Researchers disclosed "Ghostcommit," an attack that hides prompt-injection instructions inside a PNG so AI code reviewers, which treat images as opaque binary blobs, wave the malicious pull request through. Once merged, an AGENTS.md convention points a coding agent to the image, which instructs it to read .env and emit the repository's secrets as an encoded integer constant; CodeRabbit, Bugbot, and Cursor driving Claude Sonnet all missed or executed it.

title: Compromised jscrambler 8.14.0 npm Release Drops Rust Infostealer During Install
blurb: A compromised jscrambler 8.14.0 npm release, pushed July 11 under a legitimate maintainer account and absent from the project's GitHub source, uses a preinstall hook to drop and run a native Rust infostealer built for Windows, macOS, and Linux. The payload sweeps cloud credentials, crypto wallets, browser secrets, and AI coding-tool configs holding API keys and MCP credentials, and on Linux can load an eBPF program directly into the kernel.

title: Lending protocol Bonzo loses 77% of value locked as $9 million oracle exploit rattles Hedera
blurb: An attacker drained roughly $9.05 million from Bonzo Lend, a lending protocol on Hedera, by exploiting a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle contract to submit a manipulated SAUCE price update and borrow far beyond deposited collateral. Bonzo's total value locked fell 77% and Hedera's dropped nearly 40% within 24 hours, though a white-hat wallet that borrowed about $1 million during the incident has offered to return the funds.

title: Unauthenticated RCE in Motorola's MR2600 Router
blurb: Researcher MrBruh disclosed an unauthenticated remote code execution flaw in Motorola's MR2600 Wi-Fi 5 router, chaining two firmware-update endpoints that were meant to require authentication but didn't. The upload handler validates a SEAMA image by checking the multipart body's first four bytes for the magic header, so an attacker who sends the raw firmware without multipart boundaries bypasses the check and flashes malicious firmware. The last firmware, v1.0.22, shipped in mid-2024.

title: Hackers Weaponize Balochistan Police Portal in Multi-Group Espionage Campaigns
blurb: SentinelOne disclosed a sustained cyber espionage campaign against Pakistani law enforcement, including Balochistan Police servers holding biometric, criminal, and personnel records, run by suspected China- and India-aligned actors from February 2024 to April 2026. Four clusters deployed PlugX, ShadowPad, Cobalt Strike, and Remcos RAT, with one China-nexus actor planting a custom implant disguised as an update to the citizen-facing Complaint Management System.

title: Since Chromium 148, Math.tanh is now fingerprintable to link underlying OS
blurb: Since Chrome 148, a single `Math.tanh` call on the right input leaks the host OS, because V8 commit c1486295ae5 swapped its bundled fdlibm port for `std::tanh`, which routes to the platform libm and returns different last-place bits on glibc, Apple's libsystem_m, and Windows UCRT. The same divergence hits CSS trig functions and the Web Audio compressor, giving anti-bot systems a signal that contradicts a spoofed User-Agent whenever the math bits and the claimed OS disagree.

title: Misconfigured Server Reveals Three Evilginx Phishing Operations Targeting Microsoft 365
blurb: French security firm Lexfo mapped three Microsoft 365 phishing operations after one operator left directory listing enabled on a live attack server (185.163.204[.]7, Budapest), exposing phishing configs, credential logs, combolists, and Telegram session files that pivoted investigators to two more operators. All three run custom Evilginx AiTM forks but defeat MFA two mechanically distinct ways, one proxying the live login and one abusing a legitimate Microsoft sign-in flow, so M365 defenders need separate mitigations for each.

title: What xAI's Grok build CLI sends to xAI: A wire-level analysis
blurb: A wire-level teardown of xAI's official Grok Build CLI (grok) documents that, on a normal consumer login, it transmits file contents verbatim — including a .env secrets file — to xAI via POST /v1/responses and POST /v1/storage, and uploads the entire repository as a git bundle independent of what the agent reads, recovering a file it was told not to open. Captures land in the GCS bucket grok-code-session-traces, are on by default, and persist after disabling "Improve the model.

title: Fake Go DNS scanner spread malware through over 200 GitHub repos — 'Operation Muck and Load' has published 700 malicious modules since January
blurb: Socket traced a fake Go DNS scanner that acts as a first-stage Windows loader to 222 GitHub repositories across 190 accounts, part of a campaign it tracks as "Operation Muck and Load" that has pushed over 700 malicious module versions since January. The loader runs hidden PowerShell to pull certutil-decoded payloads (Vidar, RATs, XMRig) from dead drops on Pastebin, Telegram, and YouTube; Go's security team has blocked the module from its proxy.

title: Organizations Warned of Exploited Joomla Extension Vulnerabilities
blurb: Two Joomla extension flaws, Balbooa Forms (CVE-2026-56291, CVSS 10) and iCagenda (CVE-2026-48939, CVSS 10), are being exploited in the wild as zero-days, both unauthenticated arbitrary file uploads that let attackers upload PHP and achieve RCE. Patches shipped in Balbooa Forms 2.4.1 and iCagenda 4.0.8/3.9.15, and CISA added both to its KEV catalog on July 10, so administrators should patch immediately and hunt for uploaded webshells.

title: Ghost Accounts Abuse GitHub API in Mass Recon Campaign
blurb: Threat actors are abusing GitHub's largely unauthenticated API to enumerate organizations, members, and repositories, using over 50 dormant "ghost" accounts registered two to five years ago, Datadog reports. The recon requests return HTTP 200s with no auth-failure signals and blend with normal traffic, but some campaigns escalated to cloning repos and exfiltrating data, so defenders should hunt for anomalous user-agent naming on actions touching private repositories.

title: Australia warns of global campaign targeting vulnerable CMS platforms
blurb: Australia's ACSC warned that a global campaign is exploiting known CMS and plugin vulnerabilities across WordPress, Craft CMS, Joomla JCE, and others to deploy webshells, with many small- and medium-sized Australian businesses already compromised. Patch affected CMS, themes, and plugins, remove unused components, make web directories read-only, and monitor for unauthorized file creation and unexpected child-process spawning.

title: RedHook Android malware now uses Wireless ADB for shell access
blurb: A new RedHook Android banking trojan variant, analyzed by Group-IB, abuses Wireless ADB to gain shell-level (UID 2000) privileges without a rooted device or a computer connection. After tricking victims into granting Accessibility permissions, it enables Wireless Debugging, self-pairs via the loopback interface, and uses a Shizuku-based framework to run 53 server-issued commands including screen streaming, keylogging, credential theft, and silent app installation.

title: Forget typosquatting; slopsquatting is the software supply chain threat created by AI coding tools
blurb: Slopsquatting is a supply-chain attack that weaponizes LLM hallucinations: AI coding assistants invent plausible-sounding package names, attackers register those names on public registries and populate them with malware. Because these are novel invented names rather than misspellings of real libraries, registry typosquatting defenses like blocking "crossenv" against "cross-env" don't catch them. Defenders should pin and vet AI-suggested dependencies before install, and validate that every package an assistant recommends actually exists and is legitimately maintained.

title: Cynative: Open-source deep research agent
blurb: Cynative, an open-source security research agent, orchestrates a frontier model against a company's own code, cloud, and runtime, running generated code in a sandbox and classifying every action as read or write before attaching credentials, with writes gated behind explicit opt-in. Read/write mappings resolve from providers' live sources (AWS Service Reference API, iam-dataset fallback) rather than a hand-maintained table, and any unresolvable action is denied outright, so an unmapped write never slips through.

title: iCagenda and Balbooa Forms Joomla Flaws Reportedly Exploited as Zero-Days
blurb: **CISA added two maximum-severity Joomla extension flaws, CVE-2026-48939 in iCagenda and CVE-2026-56291 in Balbooa Forms (both CVSS 10.0), to its KEV catalog after zero-day exploitation enabling unauthenticated file upload and remote code execution.** Patches shipped in iCagenda 4.0.8/3.9.15 and Balbooa Forms 2.4.1; site owners should hunt for planted PHP shells in the attachments and uploads folders.

title: Progress Prompts ShareFile Storage Zone Controller Shutdown Amid Security Concerns
blurb: Progress Software told ShareFile customers to manually shut down their Storage Zone Controllers after detecting a "credible external security threat" targeting the on-premises storage component, and has temporarily disabled ShareFile account access as it investigates. Users suspect exploitation of CVE-2026-2699 (CVSS 9.8) and CVE-2026-2701 (CVSS 9.1), two March-patched flaws that chain to unauthenticated RCE, so unpatched controllers should stay offline until Progress confirms a fix.

title: Centers Laboratory Data Breach Affects 540,000 Individuals
blurb: Centers Laboratory, a New Jersey healthcare diagnostics provider, disclosed a breach affecting 542,377 individuals after WorldLeaks actors accessed its systems between August 9 and 14, 2025 and exfiltrated names, SSNs, driver's license and passport numbers, and medical and health insurance data. WorldLeaks, the extortion-only successor to Hunters International, leaked over 1.6 million files totaling 720 GB after listing the org in October 2025.

title: Why SBOMs, signing, and provenance still don’t tell you if software is safe
blurb: SBOMs, signing, and provenance verify what software contains and where it came from, but not what it will do at execution. The author argues this composition-and-authenticity trust model breaks down as AI generates and mutates code faster than human review, citing University of Toronto research on an AI-powered worm that reasons through new attack paths mid-spread. The defensible gap is behavior: teams need runtime capability analysis, not just provenance.

title: A hardware security AI assistant that checks chips for hidden backdoors
blurb: Researchers at the University of Florida released VeriChat, a conversational assistant that helps hardware security engineers hunt for hidden circuits in third-party IP blocks. It uses a three-agent retrieval pipeline over 28,221 papers to curb hallucinated answers and can run syntax and synthesis verification on an uploaded design file. In a demo, it walked a user through uncovering a Trojan planted in an AES S-Box that leaked the encryption key on a specific input trigger.

title: 99.9% of fixable AI vulnerabilities remain unpatched
blurb: Orca Security's 2026 State of AI Security Report found that 99.9% of AI vulnerability alerts with an available fix remain unpatched, and 81.2% of companies running AI packages carry at least one known vulnerability. Attackers are targeting five layers of the AI stack: package registries, model hubs, developer tools, agent frameworks, and brand trust. Prioritize patching critical CVEs (present in 74.1% of AI-package deployments) and inventory every production agent as a non-human identity with its own permissions and blast radius.

title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
blurb: FFmpeg patched PixelSmash (CVE-2026-8461), a high-severity heap out-of-bounds write in the MagicYUV decoder that JFrog used to achieve remote code execution on a Jellyfin media server through its normal library-scan pipeline. Any application built on libavcodec is exposed, including Kodi, OBS Studio, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and Linux desktop thumbnail generators, so IT teams should prioritize updating FFmpeg across self-hosted media and file-preview infrastructure.

title: Securing agentic AI with perimeter guardrails: What's new in VPC Service Controls
blurb: Google Cloud added agentic AI controls to VPC Service Controls, letting enterprises treat AI agents as first-class IAM identities in perimeter ingress and egress rules and write conditional access policies on MCP attributes like tool name and read-only status. The update also natively integrates VPC-SC with the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, automatically blocking public internet access to protected agent instances without extra configuration.

title: Irish datacenters now guzzle 23% of the country's electricity
blurb: Datacenters consumed 23% of Ireland's total electricity in 2025, up from 5% a decade ago, as hyperscale AI and cloud buildouts strain a grid already warning of supply shortfalls. Enterprises running Irish capacity face tightening constraints, with the grid operator pausing new Dublin-area connections and pushing operators toward on-site generation and stricter efficiency terms.

title: 챗GPT 워크 출시의 의미…’비용 효율 경쟁’ 시대로 재편되는 기업용 AI 판도
blurb: OpenAI launched ChatGPT Work, an enterprise agent platform that combines ChatGPT, Codex, and the newly general-available GPT-5.6 to automate multi-step tasks across connected business applications under enterprise governance. GPT-5.6 ships in three priced tiers, Sol, Terra, and Luna, with the flagship Sol at $5 per million input tokens and $30 per million output, as OpenAI pivots its enterprise pitch from benchmark wins to performance per dollar.

title: Microsoft is losing the battle to protect license lucre. It better get used to the feeling
blurb: The author argues Microsoft can no longer defend the high-margin licensing revenue that has anchored its business, as customers shift toward AI tooling, open ecosystems, and consumption-based models it doesn't control. For enterprise buyers, the takeaway is leverage: the pricing power that made Windows and Office seat licenses non-negotiable is eroding, and the piece contends Microsoft will have to accept thinner license lock-in as the new baseline.

title: Copilot could soon help diagnose issues with your PC
blurb: Microsoft is testing PC Insights, an opt-in Copilot feature that answers natural-language questions about a PC's hardware by reading CPU, RAM, GPU, and storage usage, connected devices, and Bluetooth and Wi-Fi state. The tool only flags problems rather than fixing them, and its pitch is undercut by the Copilot app itself, which Windows Latest measured using up to 1GB of RAM at idle.

title: We scaled PgBouncer to 4x throughput
blurb: ClickHouse's Managed Postgres team describes running a fleet of PgBouncer processes bound to the same port with so_reuseport, since PgBouncer is single-threaded and pins one core no matter how many the box has. On a 16-vCPU c7i.4xlarge, the fleet hit roughly 336k transactions/sec against a single process's 87k peak, about 4x. The write-up covers the operational catch: cancel requests can land on the wrong process, fixed by peering, with the connection budget split across the fleet so Postgres is never oversubscribed.

title: ZeroFS vs. Amazon S3 Files
blurb: ZeroFS and Amazon S3 Files both expose POSIX filesystems over object storage, but split on whether the bucket stays usable as plain S3. S3 Files preserves one-file, one-object identity and exports asynchronously after 60 seconds of inactivity, while ZeroFS packs, compresses, and client-side encrypts extents into opaque segments, trading direct S3 access for cheaper small-file writes.

title: Mesh LLM: distributed AI computing on iroh
blurb: Mesh LLM pools GPUs across the machines a team already owns and exposes them as one OpenAI-compatible API, routing each request to run locally, hand off to a peer with the model loaded, or split a too-large model across several boxes as a pipeline. Built on iroh endpoints with a pluggable plugin architecture, it ships a catalog of 40+ models spanning laptop-sized 500M weights up to 235B mixture-of-experts giants.

title: Securing agentic AI with perimeter guardrails: What's new in VPC Service Controls
blurb: Google Cloud added agentic-AI capabilities to VPC Service Controls, letting teams treat agents as first-class IAM identities in ingress and egress rules and write conditional access policies on MCP attributes like mcp.toolName and mcp.tool.isReadOnly. The update also natively integrates the perimeter with the Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform, automatically blocking public internet access to protected instances and letting operators revoke a compromised agent's access at the network boundary.

title: Inside a Kubernetes Bug Fix: Investigating an Integer Overflow in Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA)
blurb: A contributor documents finding and fixing an integer overflow in Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation, where arithmetic during resource range alignment could overflow near math.MaxInt64 and produce incorrect, hard-to-detect behavior. The proposed fix uses saturating arithmetic, clamping to math.MaxInt64 when a calculation would exceed the 64-bit limit, plus edge-case tests near the maximum range to guard against regressions. The post doubles as a walkthrough of navigating a large codebase before touching it.

title: Hedera lending protocol Bonzo Lend hit for $9 million after Supra verifier accepts manipulated price update
blurb: Bonzo Lend, a lending protocol on Hedera, lost $9 million after an attacker fed a manipulated price update through a Supra oracle verifier that accepted it without rejecting the forged data. A second wallet borrowed roughly $1 million during the exploit but identified itself as a white hat and said it would return the funds.

title: Bitcoin’s BIP 110 fork deadline nears with miner support at zero
blurb: Bitcoin's BIP-110, the Reduced Data Temporary Soft Fork, faces an early August activation deadline with miner support still below 1% and node adoption stuck in the low single digits. The proposal would cap OP_RETURN and block most arbitrary data pushes above 256 bytes for one year, but with Michael Saylor and Adam Back opposed, it now looks headed for a small minority chain rather than a network-wide change.

title: AI found an Ethereum bug that could take validators offline, but humans had to prove it
blurb: The Ethereum Foundation ran coordinated AI agents against its gossipsub messaging layer and surfaced a remotely triggerable crash that could knock validator nodes offline, now patched and disclosed as CVE-2026-34219. Most of the effort went to human triage rather than discovery, as the agents produced convincing false positives around test-only crashes and infeasible attacks, so the Foundation now uses agents to propose suspicious sequences and keeps traditional testing and human review to validate them.

title: Lending protocol Bonzo loses 77% of value locked as $9 million oracle exploit rattles Hedera
blurb: Bonzo Lend, a decentralized lending protocol on Hedera, lost roughly $9.05 million after an attacker exploited a verification flaw in a third-party Supra oracle, depositing near-worthless SAUCE tokens and submitting a manipulated price update to borrow far beyond their collateral. Bonzo's TVL fell 77% and Hedera's network-wide TVL dropped nearly 40% in the 24 hours after the exploit, leaving the chain at $25.7 million locked.

title: Bitcoin, ether ETFs snap eight-week outflow streaks with $282 million combined inflow
blurb: Bitcoin and ether ETFs recorded a combined $282 million in weekly inflows, ending eight-week outflow streaks that had drained $9.46 billion from the two groups. The rebound recovered only about 3% of those losses, leaving net positioning well below where it stood before the eight-week drawdown began.

title: Ripple once weighed shutting down and handing XRP to shareholders, CEO says
blurb: Ripple CEO Brad Garlinghouse said the company seriously considered shutting down and distributing its XRP holdings to shareholders on a pro rata basis rather than fighting the SEC's 2020 lawsuit. Ripple instead spent about $150 million over four years to litigate, eventually winning a ruling that XRP itself is not a security and settling the case in 2025.

title: The most underrated token type
blurb: Arcade tokens are digital currencies meant to be spent inside a specific app or product ecosystem, with supply and demand managed to hold a relatively stable value. This a16z post frames them as the on-chain version of airline miles, credit card points, and in-game gold, but open, interoperable, and composable rather than siloed in a company database. It covers where their value comes from, how builders can deploy them, and the design tradeoffs involved.

title: Japan’s Lawson convenience store pilots stablecoin payments with JPYC: report
blurb: Lawson, Japan's third-largest convenience store chain, will pilot payments in JPYC, the country's first registered yen stablecoin, at its Takanawa Gateway City store in Tokyo starting early August. The proof-of-concept runs with telecom operator KDDI and wallet firm HashPort, testing JPYC, which holds a 1:1 yen peg backed by fiat and government bonds across Avalanche, Ethereum, Polygon, and Kaia.

title: Bank of Thailand audits high-volume stablecoin trades to crack down on illicit finance: report
blurb: The Bank of Thailand and SEC have reportedly begun deploying data analytics tools to audit high-volume stablecoin trades, focusing on USDT flows tied to illicit finance. The scrutiny signals tighter oversight of Thailand's stablecoin market, where USDT dominates on-chain settlement and cross-border transfers that regulators have struggled to trace.

title: Japan’s SBI Group to launch JPYSC stablecoin lending service this month
blurb: SBI Group will launch a JPYSC stablecoin lending service in Japan this month, offering users a 3% annual yield on deposited JPYSC, according to Nikkei. The product gives a regulated yen-pegged stablecoin an on-chain lending venue under one of Japan's largest financial groups, extending domestic stablecoin infrastructure beyond payments into interest-bearing deposits.

title: Crypto IPO market stalls as capital rotates to AI and macro uncertainty weighs
blurb: The crypto IPO pipeline has stalled as capital rotates into AI and macro uncertainty erodes appetite for new listings, according to Cohen & Company's Christian Lopez, who cites funding constraints and investor caution over regulation as the cause. Kraken parent Payward, Consensys, Ledger, and Grayscale have all delayed offerings after weak post-listing performances from Circle, Bullish, and BitGo cooled the 2026 outlook.

title: A look at ADI Predictstreet's troubled launch, marked by tiny trading volumes, withdrawal bugs, and other issues, as it tried to challenge Kalshi and Polymarket (Wall Street Journal)
blurb: ADI's Predictstreet launched to minuscule trading volumes, withdrawal bugs, and other technical failures in its bid to compete with prediction-market incumbents Kalshi and Polymarket. The rough debut underscores how hard liquidity and reliable settlement are to bootstrap, leaving the two established venues in control of the market.

title: The Funding: Why Japan’s SBI Holdings is investing big in crypto
blurb: SBI Holdings, one of Japan's largest financial groups, has accelerated its crypto push through a series of deals spanning custody, stablecoins, and exchange operations, positioning itself as a bridge between traditional finance and digital assets. The strategy reflects a broader pattern of established institutions building crypto infrastructure through acquisitions and partnerships rather than launching products from scratch.

title: Crypto fatwa sparks debate over Pakistan’s digital asset framework
blurb: Pakistan's crypto regulator, PVARA Chairman Bilal bin Saqib, met with Islamic scholar Mufti Taqi Usmani after a June fatwa declared cryptocurrencies impermissible under Sharia and invalidated purchases made with USDT and other tokens. Saqib argued that stablecoins, tokenized real-world assets, and blockchains warrant separate technical and Sharia assessments rather than a blanket ruling, as Pakistan builds a framework covering a sovereign stablecoin, state-asset tokenization, and exchange licensing.

title: Michael Saylor posts cryptic Strategy chart after $216 million bitcoin sale: ‘Orange dots tell only part of the story’
blurb: Michael Saylor posted Strategy's bitcoin acquisition tracker with the caption "Orange dots tell only part of the story," a departure from his usual purchase hints that followed the company's record $216 million sale of 3,588 BTC last week. Strategy now holds 843,775 BTC at an average cost of $75,476, leaving the position roughly $9.7 billion underwater with bitcoin trading near $64,000.

title: UPI: Anatomy of a Payment Transaction
blurb: UPI processed more than 2,272 crore payments in June 2026, more than any other real-time payment system in the world, yet the app you tap is only the first of seven parties in the chain and never touches your money. This piece traces a single transaction from scan to green tick, detailing what each layer checks and where it fails. It also shows the app tier stays a duopoly, with PhonePe and Google Pay together carrying roughly four-fifths of volume.

title: Moneybox crowned Europe’s newest unicorn as it gears up to test London’s Pisces market
blurb: Moneybox reached a $1.1B valuation and joined Europe's unicorn ranks as it prepares a £45M secondary sale for long-tenured employees on LSEG's new Pisces-framework Private Securities Market. The move makes Moneybox one of the first fintechs to test Pisces, the UK's bid to keep homegrown startups liquid at home after Revolut, OakNorth, and others weighed listing abroad.

title: German Tax Trades Fallout May Cost Firms €7 Billion, BaFin Says
blurb: BaFin said financial firms that used two dividend-arbitrage strategies to game German tax rules now face combined legal and fiscal liabilities of up to €7.01 billion ($7.99 billion). The estimate signals the regulator intends to pursue clawbacks aggressively, raising the cost of the cum-ex and cum-cum trades for banks and trading firms that structured them.

title: Revolut to launch US bank in 2027, says US boss
blurb: Revolut plans to launch a standalone US bank in 2027, headquartered in Stamford, Connecticut, pending approval of the OCC and FDIC banking licence it applied for in March. A charter would let the $75B neobank drop its partner-bank arrangements and earn credit interchange directly, offering FDIC-insured deposits, credit cards, and crypto access to its one million-plus US retail customers.

title: Japan’s Lawson convenience store pilots stablecoin payments with JPYC: report
blurb: Lawson, Japan's third-largest convenience store chain, will begin accepting the yen-pegged stablecoin JPYC at its Takanawa Gateway City store in Tokyo from early August, part of a proof-of-concept with telecom operator KDDI and wallet firm HashPort. JPYC is Japan's first licensed yen stablecoin under the Payment Services Act, fully backed by yen deposits and government bonds, giving the pilot a regulated settlement rail rather than an offshore token.

title: Japan’s SBI Group to launch JPYSC stablecoin lending service this month
blurb: SBI Group will launch a JPYSC stablecoin lending service in Japan this month, offering users a 3% annual yield on deposited tokens, according to Nikkei. The product extends Japan's regulated stablecoin framework beyond payments into yield-bearing deposits, giving SBI an early position in domestic stablecoin infrastructure as banks and fintechs compete to define lending rails around the yen-pegged token.

title: The Funding: Why Japan’s SBI Holdings is investing big in crypto
blurb: SBI Holdings, one of Japan's largest financial groups, has accelerated its crypto investments through a string of recent deals spanning trading infrastructure, custody, and stablecoin settlement. The pattern shows how a regulated incumbent is building digital-asset exposure via acquisitions and partnerships rather than in-house builds, a template other traditional-finance players are likely to follow as Japan's rules clarify.

title: MUFG Surpasses Toyota to Become Japan’s Most Valuable Firm
blurb: Mitsubishi UFJ Financial Group surpassed Toyota to become Japan's most valuable listed company, the first time a bank has held the top spot since the current three-megabank structure formed. The move reflects a broad repricing of Japanese lenders as the Bank of Japan's exit from negative rates restores net interest margins that had been compressed for nearly a decade.

title: Fintechs face an ‘existential threat’ that excludes them from the CGT fix for startups
blurb: FinTech Australia told Treasury that almost all fintech startups would be ineligible for the proposed Innovative Business CGT Concession, the carve-out meant to shield startups from May's budget changes that replace the 50% CGT discount with cost-base indexation and a 30% minimum tax from July 2027. The industry body calls the exclusion an existential threat to a sector contributing $13.6B in direct economic value.
